channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Due Diligence Fund (DDF) 5th Call for Proposals 2026: Up to €250,000 Grants for Sustainable Agricultural Supply Chain Projects
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Due Diligence Fund (DDF) 5th Call for Proposals 2026 is now open, offering significant funding opportunities for innovative partnerships working to strengthen human rights and environmental due diligence (HREDD) in global agricultural supply chains. Implemented on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ) and managed by GIZ, this fund […]

The post Due Diligence Fund (DDF) 5th Call for Proposals 2026: Up to €250,000 Grants for Sustainable Agricultural Supply Chain Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Due Diligence Fund (DDF) 5th Call for Proposals 2026 is now open, offering significant funding opportunities for innovative partnerships working to strengthen human rights and environmental due diligence (HREDD) in global agricultural supply chains. Implemented on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ) and managed by GIZ, this fund supports transformative, scalable, and practical solutions that go beyond compliance to create sustainable and ethical supply chains.

With grants of up to €250,000 available, this initiative aims to support impactful projects that improve working conditions, protect the environment, and promote inclusive development—particularly in agricultural sectors across developing countries.

About the Due Diligence Fund (DDF)

The Due Diligence Fund is a strategic funding mechanism designed to promote responsible business practices in global agricultural supply chains. It focuses on enabling companies and organizations to implement effective due diligence systems that address environmental risks and human rights concerns.

Core Objectives of the DDF

  • Promote innovative and practical HREDD approaches
  • Strengthen sustainable agricultural supply chains
  • Improve living and working conditions of primary producers
  • Encourage partnerships between private sector and non-profits
  • Generate scalable and transferable solutions

What is New in the 5th Call (DDF5)

The fifth funding round builds on lessons learned from previous cycles and introduces an enhanced funding structure.

Key Highlights:

  • Two funding windows available
  • Grants of up to €250,000 per project
  • Focus on larger, more comprehensive initiatives
  • Strengthened emphasis on scalability and innovation
  • Alignment with global and EU due diligence regulations

What the Fund Offers

Selected projects will receive a comprehensive support package beyond financial assistance.

1. Financial Support

  • Grants of up to €250,000
  • Co-financing required from EU-based companies (depending on size)

2. Technical Assistance

  • Guidance throughout project development and implementation
  • Support from experts in HREDD and supply chain sustainability

3. Global Network Access

  • Connection to international partners and stakeholders
  • Access to industry expertise and knowledge-sharing platforms

4. Visibility and Knowledge Sharing

  • Public dissemination of project findings
  • Contribution to industry-wide learning and best practices

Focus Areas and Thematic Priorities

The DDF supports projects addressing critical issues in agricultural supply chains, including:

  • Deforestation-free supply chains
  • Climate change and biodiversity protection
  • Digital inclusion in agriculture
  • Gender equality and social inclusion
  • Living incomes and fair wages

Special emphasis is placed on projects supporting gender equality, in recognition of the International Year of the Woman Farmer.

Why Human Rights and Environmental Due Diligence (HREDD) Matters

Global agricultural supply chains are increasingly required to address environmental and human rights risks. International frameworks and regulations such as:

  • UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights
  • OECD Guidelines for Responsible Business Conduct
  • German Supply Chain Due Diligence Act (LkSG)
  • EU Directive on Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence (CSDDD)
  • EU Regulation on Deforestation-Free Products (EUDR)

have made due diligence a critical component of responsible business operations.

Benefits of Effective HREDD

  • Improved transparency across supply chains
  • Stronger relationships with suppliers
  • Enhanced access to global markets
  • Increased resilience and competitiveness
  • Sustainable production practices

Types of Projects Supported

The DDF seeks projects that are:

  • Innovative and practical
  • Scalable and transferable
  • Focused on primary production
  • Designed to address real-world risks

Key Project Requirements:

  • Demonstrate potential to reduce negative impacts
  • Use a risk-based approach
  • Follow a process-oriented methodology
  • Go beyond minimum legal requirements
  • Promote gender equality and inclusion

Programme Structure and Selection Process

The DDF application process consists of multiple stages:

1. Submission of Short Proposal

  • Outline project concept and partnership structure

2. Shortlisting

  • Selected applicants invited to submit a detailed proposal

3. Long Proposal Submission

  • Comprehensive project plan with implementation strategy

4. Final Selection

  • Projects evaluated and selected by GIZ and BMZ
  • Successful applicants receive funding and technical support

Eligibility Criteria

Applications must be submitted by partnerships that include:

Required Partners:

  1. At least one company based in:
    • European Union (EU)
    • European Economic Area (EEA)
    • Switzerland or the United Kingdom

AND

  1. At least one of the following:
    • Company in a DAC-listed country
    • Non-profit organization with relevant expertise

Additional Requirements:

  • Private companies must be active in agricultural supply chains
  • Co-financing required from European companies
  • Strong collaboration between partners

How to Apply

The application process is structured into three steps:

Step 1: Review Application Documents

Applicants must carefully review:

  • Terms and Conditions
  • Application Templates:
    • Template A (Applicant Information)
    • Template B (Concept Note)

Step 2: Prepare Application

  • Use supporting materials:
    • FAQ document
    • Application checklist
  • Attend optional Q&A sessions

Step 3: Submit Application

  • Submit all documents via email to: ddf@giz.de
  • Details on submission can be found in the Application Templates

Application Deadline:

June 15, 2026 (11:59 PM CEST)

Q&A Sessions (Optional Support)

Applicants can join live Q&A sessions on:

  • April 16
  • April 22
  • April 30
  • May 6

These sessions provide guidance on improving application quality. Click here to register.

Programme Impact

The DDF has already supported:

  • 30 projects across 4 funding rounds
  • Innovative approaches to sustainable agriculture
  • Practical solutions benefiting supply chain actors

Click HERE to learn more about the Due Diligence Fund (DDF) 5th Call for Proposals 2026.

For more global Grant opportunities, visit OFY HERE.

The post Due Diligence Fund (DDF) 5th Call for Proposals 2026: Up to €250,000 Grants for Sustainable Agricultural Supply Chain Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ESD Okayama Award 2026: Win $3,000 for Global Sustainable Development Projects (Apply Now)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Organizations around the world are invited to apply for the ESD Okayama Award 2026, a prestigious global recognition celebrating impactful initiatives in Education for Sustainable Development (ESD). If your project is advancing sustainability and contributing to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), this is an opportunity to gain international recognition and support. About the ESD Okayama […]

The post ESD Okayama Award 2026: Win $3,000 for Global Sustainable Development Projects (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations around the world are invited to apply for the ESD Okayama Award 2026, a prestigious global recognition celebrating impactful initiatives in Education for Sustainable Development (ESD). If your project is advancing sustainability and contributing to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), this is an opportunity to gain international recognition and support.

About the ESD Okayama Award

Launched in 2015 by the City of Okayama and the ESD Okayama Award Steering Board, the award honors outstanding ESD projects from across the globe.

Its mission is to:

  • Recognize innovative and impactful ESD initiatives
  • Support the growth of award-winning organizations
  • Promote global awareness and adoption of ESD practices
  • Contribute to achieving the SDGs

Who Can Apply?

Applications are open to:

  • Organizations worldwide
  • Projects focused on Education for Sustainable Development
  • Initiatives that have been running for at least 3 years (as of April 2026)

Award Details

  • Number of Winners: 1–2 projects
  • Prize: USD $3,000 per project
  • Additional Opportunity:
    Winners will be invited to Okayama to:
    • Attend the award ceremony
    • Present their project
    • Network with local organizations

Key Dates

  • Application Deadline: 14 May 2026 (5:00 PM JST)
  • Award Ceremony: 14 November 2026

How to Apply

Follow these steps to submit your application:

  1. Temporary Registration
    Register your organization’s email to receive access to the application portal
  2. Complete the Application Form
    Fill out the official online form provided via email
  3. Receive Confirmation
    You will get an automated acknowledgment of your submission
  4. Final Confirmation
    The secretariat will notify you once your application is complete

Before applying, review the Application Guide and prepare supporting materials such as documents or photos.

Why You Should Apply

  • Gain international recognition for your work
  • Receive funding to support your initiative
  • Showcase your project on a global platform
  • Build connections with sustainability leaders

Pro Tip

Focus on clearly demonstrating your project’s long-term impact, measurable outcomes, and how it contributes to the SDGs. Strong storytelling combined with data can significantly boost your chances of success.

Learn More

You can explore past winners and additional details through the official website of the City of Okayama.

Discover similar opportunities here. 

The post ESD Okayama Award 2026: Win $3,000 for Global Sustainable Development Projects (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Black Teacher Wellness Grants 2026: $500 Funding Opportunity for Educator Wellbeing in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Black Teacher Wellness Grants 2026 provide targeted funding to support the mental health, self-care, and overall wellbeing of Black TK–12 educators across the United States. This opportunity is designed for teachers who are navigating demanding educational environments and are seeking resources to prioritize their personal wellness. With grants of up to $500, the program […]

The post Black Teacher Wellness Grants 2026: $500 Funding Opportunity for Educator Wellbeing in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Black Teacher Wellness Grants 2026 provide targeted funding to support the mental health, self-care, and overall wellbeing of Black TK–12 educators across the United States. This opportunity is designed for teachers who are navigating demanding educational environments and are seeking resources to prioritize their personal wellness. With grants of up to $500, the program offers direct support to educators committed to sustaining their wellbeing while continuing to make a meaningful impact in their classrooms and communities.

About the Program

The Black Teacher Wellness Grants are an initiative of the Black Teacher Project (BTP), an organization dedicated to building a strong and thriving community of Black educators. The program recognizes the critical role Black teachers play in shaping educational outcomes and fostering inclusive learning environments.

This grant cycle is dedicated to the legacy of Dr. Marvin Boomer, an educator whose work exemplified the importance of community, leadership, and wellbeing. His approach emphasized that teacher wellness is not a luxury, but a foundational element for effective teaching and long-term impact.

The grants are specifically designed to support personal wellness activities outside the classroom. This may include mental health services, wellness programs, self-care practices, or other activities that help educators restore and sustain their physical and emotional wellbeing.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Black educators often face unique challenges within the education system, including disproportionate workloads, lack of culturally responsive support, and systemic inequities. These pressures can lead to burnout, stress, and long-term health impacts.

Research consistently shows that Black teachers have a significant positive influence on student outcomes, particularly for Black students. However, despite their impact, they often operate in environments that do not adequately support their wellbeing.

This grant addresses that gap by providing direct financial support for self-care and healing. By investing in teacher wellness, the program contributes to stronger, more resilient educators who are better equipped to support their students and communities.

Ultimately, supporting the wellbeing of Black teachers is essential not only for individual educators but for the broader goal of creating equitable and thriving education systems.

What Participants Will Gain

Selected applicants will receive grants of up to $500, which can be used to support a wide range of wellness-related activities. This financial support allows educators to access resources that may otherwise be out of reach.

Beyond the funding, the grant represents recognition and validation of the importance of teacher wellbeing. It encourages educators to prioritize their mental health, rest, and personal growth without guilt.

Participants also benefit from being part of a broader movement that centers the experiences, leadership, and wellbeing of Black educators. This connection reinforces the idea that wellness is a collective priority, not an individual burden.

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to Black TK–12 classroom teachers currently working in the United States. Applicants must identify as Black, African American, or part of the African Diaspora.

Eligible candidates must be teachers of record and actively engaged in classroom teaching. The grant is intended for individual educators, not organizations or student-focused projects.

Previous recipients of the Spring 2024, Winter 2024, or Spring 2025 Black Teacher Project grants are not eligible to apply for this cycle.

Eligible Areas

This opportunity is open to applicants across the United States. Applicants must be able to receive payment within the U.S., as international payments are not supported.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants must complete the online application form, which takes approximately one hour to complete. The application includes questions about the applicant’s background, teaching experience, and intended use of the grant for wellness purposes.

The application timeline is as follows:
Applications close on 15 April 2026. Following the review period, selected applicants will be notified between late April and early May, with public announcements expected in May 2026.

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and provide clear, thoughtful responses that demonstrate how the grant will support their wellbeing.

Learn more and apply in the official Google Application Form.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Black Teacher Wellness Grants 2026: $500 Funding Opportunity for Educator Wellbeing in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EU Gender Action Plan 2026: Submit Your Feedback to Shape Global Equality Efforts
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The EU Gender Action Plan (GAP IV) consultation is now open, offering researchers, youth, and policy professionals a unique opportunity to shape the future of gender equality in global development. This initiative invites stakeholders worldwide to contribute evidence and insights that will inform the European Union’s external action on gender equality and women’s empowerment from […]

The post EU Gender Action Plan 2026: Submit Your Feedback to Shape Global Equality Efforts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The EU Gender Action Plan (GAP IV) consultation is now open, offering researchers, youth, and policy professionals a unique opportunity to shape the future of gender equality in global development. This initiative invites stakeholders worldwide to contribute evidence and insights that will inform the European Union’s external action on gender equality and women’s empowerment from 2028 to 2034. If you are working in research, innovation, policy, or advocacy, this is a critical chance to influence international priorities and ensure gender equality is embedded across global partnerships.

About the Program

The EU Gender Action Plan IV (GAP IV) is a forthcoming policy framework that will guide how the European Union promotes gender equality and women’s empowerment in its external relations. Building on previous action plans, GAP IV aims to strengthen the EU’s global leadership in advancing gender equality across development cooperation, humanitarian aid, trade, and international partnerships.

As part of its development, the European Commission has launched a Call for Evidence, inviting contributions from individuals, organizations, researchers, and institutions. These inputs will help refine priorities, identify gaps, and ensure that the final strategy reflects diverse perspectives and real-world challenges.

A key focus of the consultation is the role of science, technology, and innovation (STI) partnerships in advancing gender equality. Current evidence shows that many international collaborations remain “gender blind,” lacking adequate monitoring, accountability, and inclusive design. GAP IV aims to address these shortcomings by embedding gender equality more systematically into global research and innovation systems.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Gender equality remains a fundamental human right and a cornerstone of sustainable development. Despite progress over recent decades, significant inequalities persist across social, economic, and political systems worldwide. These disparities are often amplified in fragile and conflict-affected contexts.

This consultation is important because it directly influences how one of the world’s largest development actors—the European Union—allocates resources, designs partnerships, and sets global priorities. Contributions to GAP IV can help ensure that gender equality is not treated as an afterthought but as a central pillar of international cooperation.

In particular, integrating gender equality into research and innovation has far-reaching implications. Evidence shows that inclusive approaches improve scientific quality, enhance societal relevance, and lead to more effective and equitable outcomes. By contributing to this consultation, stakeholders can help shape policies that promote inclusive participation, reduce structural inequalities, and strengthen global collaboration.

What Participants Will Gain

Although this is not a funded programme, participating in the consultation offers significant professional and strategic benefits.

Contributors gain the opportunity to influence high-level EU policy that will shape international development priorities for years to come. This level of engagement is particularly valuable for researchers, practitioners, and organizations seeking to align their work with global frameworks.

Participants also benefit from increased visibility within policy and development spaces, as submissions are reviewed and considered by EU institutions. Engaging in this process can strengthen credibility, expand networks, and position contributors as thought leaders in gender equality and international development.

Additionally, the process provides an opportunity to reflect on existing evidence, consolidate insights, and contribute to a broader global dialogue on gender equality, innovation, and inclusive development.

Who Can Apply

The consultation is open to a wide range of stakeholders. This includes researchers, academic institutions, civil society organizations, policymakers, private sector actors, and individuals with relevant expertise.

Participants are particularly encouraged to contribute if they have experience in gender equality, international development, research and innovation, or related fields. Submissions that include evidence-based insights, data, or practical recommendations are especially valuable.

There are no strict eligibility restrictions, making this an accessible opportunity for global participation.

Eligible Areas

This is a global opportunity, open to participants from all regions. Contributions are welcomed from individuals and organizations worldwide, regardless of geographic location.

Application Process and Deadline

To participate, contributors must submit their feedback through the European Commission’s online platform. Registration or login is required before submitting input.

Submissions should provide clear, evidence-based perspectives on gender equality, particularly in relation to international partnerships, research, and innovation.

The deadline to submit contributions is 30 April 2026 (midnight Brussels time). Early submissions are encouraged to ensure adequate time for review.

Learn more and submit your feedback on the official European Commission site.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post EU Gender Action Plan 2026: Submit Your Feedback to Shape Global Equality Efforts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: PhD on African Migrant Health at University of Oslo (HEMI Project)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The University of Oslo is offering a fully funded PhD position at the Institute of Health and Society within its Faculty of Medicine. This opportunity is part of the research project “Health and Migration (HEMI): A Multi-country Study of African Male Migrants.” The successful candidate will join an international, multidisciplinary team exploring how migration influences […]

The post Apply Now: PhD on African Migrant Health at University of Oslo (HEMI Project) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Oslo is offering a fully funded PhD position at the Institute of Health and Society within its Faculty of Medicine. This opportunity is part of the research project “Health and Migration (HEMI): A Multi-country Study of African Male Migrants.”

The successful candidate will join an international, multidisciplinary team exploring how migration influences health, vulnerability, and resilience among African sexual and gender minority men. The position is a three-year fixed-term doctoral appointment based in Norway.

About the HEMI Project

The HEMI project is funded by the Research Council of Norway and implemented in collaboration with partner universities in Africa and Europe, as well as civil society organizations.

It is affiliated with the Africa-Europe Cluster of Research Excellence in Health, Gender and Sexualities (CoRE HGS)—a network of eight universities across Africa and Europe.

The project focuses on:

  • Migration journeys from origin to destination
  • Health challenges and coping strategies among migrants
  • Access to healthcare and support systems
  • Experiences of vulnerability and resilience

Four PhD candidates will contribute to this multi-country study, with research spanning Norway, Tanzania, Zimbabwe, and South Africa.

Research Focus and Methodology

The selected PhD candidate will focus primarily on African migrants living in Norway, examining their health, well-being, and lived experiences.

The research will involve:

  • Qualitative methods such as in-depth interviews, focus group discussions, and participant observation
  • Collaboration with community organizations and stakeholders
  • Participatory and co-research approaches involving migrant communities
  • Analysis of national policies affecting migrant health

The project includes extensive fieldwork in Norway and collaboration with healthcare providers, public service workers, and civil society actors.

Academic Environment

The position is based at the Institute of Health and Society (Helsam), within the Section for Transdisciplinary Research for Sustainable Health (TRUST).

This department provides a globally oriented research environment integrating expertise from:

  • Medicine
  • Health sciences
  • Social sciences

The PhD candidate will also engage in cross-national collaboration through workshops, supervision meetings, and joint research outputs.

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will:

  • Contribute to the design of the Norwegian sub-study, including methodology and ethical approvals
  • Conduct qualitative fieldwork and data collection
  • Analyze data and contribute to theoretical development
  • Publish approximately three academic articles forming the PhD thesis
  • Participate in joint publications and international collaborations
  • Present findings at conferences, seminars, and policy forums
  • Engage actively in project workshops and meetings

Eligibility Criteria

Required Qualifications

Applicants must:

  • Hold a master’s degree (or equivalent) in public health, sociology, social anthropology, gender studies, or a related field
  • Alternatively, have a background in medicine or health sciences with an interest in social science research
  • Have an average grade of B or higher (C may be considered individually)
  • Demonstrate excellent written and spoken English
  • Possess strong academic writing and research skills
  • Be able to manage research independently and communicate complex ideas effectively

Desired Qualifications

It is advantageous to have:

  • Experience in migration research or programming
  • Experience working with sexual and gender minority populations
  • Knowledge of qualitative research methods
  • Familiarity with the Norwegian health or welfare system
  • Proficiency in Norwegian
  • Experience in international or cross-cultural research

Personal Attributes

Ideal candidates will:

  • Be motivated to conduct impactful and meaningful research
  • Work effectively both independently and in teams
  • Contribute positively to a collaborative research environment
  • Communicate openly about research progress and challenges
  • Demonstrate curiosity, engagement, and strong organizational skills

What the University Offers

The University of Oslo provides:

  • A stimulating research environment with a strong societal mission
  • Opportunities to collaborate across Africa and Europe
  • Access to an international academic network
  • Competitive salary (NOK 550,800–610,000 annually)
  • Membership in Norway’s State Pension Fund
  • Welfare benefits, insurance, and favorable mortgage schemes

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • A CV (maximum two pages)
  • Academic transcripts
  • Three academic references
  • A writing sample (4,000–10,000 words)
  • A personal statement (up to three pages) addressing motivation, experience, research interests, and career goals
  • A declaration of any AI tools used in preparing the application

Applications must be submitted through the official recruitment system.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

The University of Oslo is committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse academic environment. Applicants from underrepresented backgrounds, including those with disabilities or immigrant backgrounds, are encouraged to apply.

The institution actively promotes equal opportunities and aims to reflect a wide range of perspectives in its research community.

Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews. Applicants should be aware that, in accordance with Norwegian law, their names may appear on a public applicant list unless an exemption is granted.

This PhD position offers a unique opportunity to contribute to impactful global health research while building a strong academic career within an international network.

Discover similar opportunities Here.

The post Apply Now: PhD on African Migrant Health at University of Oslo (HEMI Project) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program 2026 at the University of Pretoria: Fully Funded Scholarships for African Students (Undergraduate & Postgraduate)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program 2026 at the University of Pretoria (UP), South Africa, is a prestigious, fully funded scholarship initiative designed to support academically talented young Africans who face economic and social barriers. This life-changing program goes beyond traditional scholarships by offering comprehensive financial support, leadership development, mentorship, and community engagement opportunities to empower […]

The post Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program 2026 at the University of Pretoria: Fully Funded Scholarships for African Students (Undergraduate & Postgraduate) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program 2026 at the University of Pretoria (UP), South Africa, is a prestigious, fully funded scholarship initiative designed to support academically talented young Africans who face economic and social barriers. This life-changing program goes beyond traditional scholarships by offering comprehensive financial support, leadership development, mentorship, and community engagement opportunities to empower the next generation of African leaders.

Since its launch in 2014, the program has supported hundreds of scholars across Africa, equipping them with the knowledge, skills, and values necessary to contribute meaningfully to the continent’s social and economic transformation. With the introduction of Phase II in December 2023, the program continues to expand its reach, offering even more opportunities for African students to access world-class education.

About the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program

The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program is a global initiative aimed at developing transformative leaders who will drive sustainable development across Africa and beyond. At the University of Pretoria, the program focuses on providing holistic support to students pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate (Honours and Master’s) degrees.

Core Objectives of the Program

  • To provide access to quality higher education for talented African students
  • To empower youth facing financial and social barriers
  • To develop leadership capacity and ethical values
  • To foster entrepreneurship, innovation, and community service
  • To create a network of alumni committed to giving back to their communities

Vision of the Program

The program envisions a future where young Africans are equipped to:

  • Contribute to economic growth and social transformation
  • Secure meaningful employment or pursue entrepreneurship
  • Lead initiatives that improve their communities and countries
  • Become part of a global network of change-makers

What the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program Covers

The scholarship provides comprehensive “wraparound support,” ensuring that scholars can fully focus on their academic and personal development.

Key Benefits Include:

  1. Full Tuition Coverage
    • Covers all academic-related costs
  2. Accommodation
    • Access to university residences across multiple campuses
  3. Living Expenses
    • Support for daily needs throughout the study period
  4. Learning and Leadership Opportunities
    • Leadership training and personal development programs
  5. Internship and Work Experience
    • Opportunities to gain practical experience
  6. Community Engagement
    • Mandatory participation in community service initiatives
  7. Entrepreneurship Support
    • Resources and training for entrepreneurial ventures
  8. Alumni Network
    • Continued support after graduation

Target Group and Inclusion Focus

The program specifically targets:

  • Academically talented young Africans
  • Individuals facing economic and social barriers
  • Students living with disabilities
  • Internally displaced persons and refugees

The goal is to ensure inclusivity while nurturing leaders who will contribute to Africa’s development.

Academic Levels Supported

The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at the University of Pretoria supports:

  • Undergraduate degrees
  • Postgraduate degrees:
    • Honours
    • Master’s

Applicants must first apply and be accepted into an eligible program at the University of Pretoria before being considered for the scholarship.

Programme Components

The program is designed to provide a holistic educational experience through:

1. Academic Excellence

  • Access to high-quality education at a leading African university
  • Support for academic success

2. Leadership Development

  • Structured leadership training
  • Exposure to global and local challenges

3. Community Service

  • Active involvement in community development initiatives
  • Opportunities to create social impact

4. Career Development

  • Internships and professional development opportunities
  • Career guidance and mentorship

5. Alumni Engagement

  • Strong alumni network
  • Commitment to giving back after graduation

Click HERE to learn more and to apply for the scholarship.

University of Pretoria Application Process

To be considered for the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program, applicants must follow the University of Pretoria’s application process.

Step-by-Step Guide:

  1. Choose a Study Programme
    • Identify a course aligned with your career goals
  2. Prepare Required Documents
    • ID or passport
    • Academic records (e.g., Grade 11 results for undergraduate applicants)
  3. Submit Online Application
    • Apply through the University of Pretoria portal
  4. Apply Early
    • Early applications improve chances of acceptance

Important Application Information

  • Applications for the 2027 academic year open: April 1, 2026
  • Deadlines vary depending on the programme (e.g., Veterinary Science and other courses)
  • Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to high competition

Accommodation and Student Life

The University of Pretoria offers a vibrant student experience with:

  • 29 residences across multiple campuses:
    • Hatfield
    • Hillcrest
    • Groenkloof
    • Prinshof
    • Onderstepoort

Students benefit from a supportive academic and social environment that enhances their overall university experience.

Financial Aid and Additional Support

In addition to the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program, the university provides:

  • Tuition discounts
  • Bursaries and financial aid options
  • Guidance on funding opportunities

Click HERE to learn more and apply to the University of Pretoria.

Why You Should Apply

The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program stands out as one of the most impactful scholarship programs in Africa.

Key Reasons:

  • Fully funded education at a top university
  • Holistic support beyond tuition
  • Strong focus on leadership and impact
  • Opportunities for internships and entrepreneurship
  • Access to a global alumni network

Discover more global Scholarship and Research opportunities on OFY.

 

The post Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program 2026 at the University of Pretoria: Fully Funded Scholarships for African Students (Undergraduate & Postgraduate) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Climate Launchpad 2026: Global Green Startup Competition (Apply by May 15)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Do you have a climate-focused business idea but don’t know where to start? The Climate Launchpad 2026 Competition is now open for applications—offering aspiring innovators the opportunity to turn early-stage ideas into impactful, scalable green startups. Whether you’re a student, entrepreneur, engineer, or simply passionate about sustainability, Climate Launchpad welcomes you—even if your idea is […]

The post Climate Launchpad 2026: Global Green Startup Competition (Apply by May 15) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Do you have a climate-focused business idea but don’t know where to start? The Climate Launchpad 2026 Competition is now open for applications—offering aspiring innovators the opportunity to turn early-stage ideas into impactful, scalable green startups.

Whether you’re a student, entrepreneur, engineer, or simply passionate about sustainability, Climate Launchpad welcomes you—even if your idea is still in its earliest form.

What is Climate Launchpad?

Climate Launchpad is the world’s largest green business ideas competition, designed to support early-stage climate innovators. The program equips participants with the skills, mentorship, and global exposure needed to launch and grow climate solutions.

Participants gain access to expert-led training, structured modules, and opportunities to pitch their ideas on both regional and global stages.

Why You Should Apply

By joining Climate Launchpad 2026, you will:

  • Develop your business and pitching skills
  • Receive hands-on training from experienced coaches
  • Build a strong startup pitch deck
  • Access a global network of innovators and investors
  • Compete for travel sponsorship and global exposure
  • Get a chance to pitch at the Global Grand Final in Singapore

Even if your idea is just a concept, this program will help you shape it into a viable business.

Climate Launchpad 2026 Timeline

Here’s a breakdown of the competition journey:

Application Deadline

May 15, 2026
All applications must be submitted via the official Climate Launchpad platform. Successful applicants will proceed to the next phase.

 May – June: Online Mini-Course

Selected applicants will participate in an online course to prepare for the Boot Camp. Performance in this stage helps determine who advances.

 June 10–12 (Tentative): Boot Camp

A virtual, expert-led training (three half-days) where participants learn how to build and refine their startup pitch decks.

June – August: Follow-Up Sessions

Participants receive ongoing support, mentorship, and Q&A sessions to strengthen their business ideas ahead of the national finals.

 August 14 (Tentative): Swiss National Final

Participants pitch their ideas to a jury.

  • Top 3 teams + 1 runner-up are selected
  • The winner receives travel sponsorship to the Global Grand Final in Singapore

 September: European Regional Final

Top teams from national competitions compete regionally.

  • The best startups from each region advance to the global stage

October 14: Global Grand Final (Singapore)

The top 8 startups worldwide pitch their ideas in Singapore for global recognition and opportunities.

November: Startup Academy Guidance Program

Winners from the Swiss National Final receive direct entry into an advanced startup support program.

Who Can Apply?

Climate Launchpad is open to:

  • Early-stage startups
  • Students and recent graduates
  • Innovators with climate-focused ideas
  • Anyone passionate about sustainability and entrepreneurship

No prior business experience required!

How to Apply

Applications are submitted through the official Climate Launchpad website. Before applying, it’s strongly recommended to review the FAQs to confirm eligibility and understand the process.

Pro Tip

Don’t wait for your idea to be “perfect.” Climate Launchpad is designed to help you build, test, and refine your concept—so apply even if it’s still rough.

Final Thoughts

Climate Launchpad 2026 is more than just a competition—it’s a launchpad for the next generation of climate entrepreneurs. If you’re ready to make a real impact and bring your green idea to life, this is your chance.

Apply before May 15, 2026, and take your first step toward building a sustainable future.

Discover similar opportunities Here.

The post Climate Launchpad 2026: Global Green Startup Competition (Apply by May 15) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Join UNICEF Nigeria’s Youth Action Team (YPAT) 2026: A Life-Changing Opportunity for Young Leaders
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The stage is set for a new generation of bold, passionate young leaders ready to make meaningful impact in their communities and beyond. If you are driven by purpose, committed to social change, and eager to grow your leadership capacity, the UNICEF GenU 9JA Young People’s Action Team (YPAT) 2026 is the opportunity you’ve been […]

The post Join UNICEF Nigeria’s Youth Action Team (YPAT) 2026: A Life-Changing Opportunity for Young Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The stage is set for a new generation of bold, passionate young leaders ready to make meaningful impact in their communities and beyond. If you are driven by purpose, committed to social change, and eager to grow your leadership capacity, the UNICEF GenU 9JA Young People’s Action Team (YPAT) 2026 is the opportunity you’ve been waiting for.

This flagship initiative by UNICEF Nigeria is designed to empower young people to actively participate in shaping policies, driving community-based projects, and influencing decisions that affect their future.

Why You Should Apply

The YPAT program is not just another volunteer opportunity—it is a transformative leadership journey. Selected participants will collaborate directly with UNICEF teams, development partners, and policymakers to address critical issues such as education, climate action, digital innovation, health, and WASH.

Participants will gain hands-on experience and contribute to real-world solutions that impact communities at local, national, and global levels.

What You’ll Gain

Joining YPAT opens the door to a wide range of personal and professional development opportunities:

  • Practical experience in youth advocacy and governance
  • Skills in project planning and community engagement
  • Training in digital content creation and social campaigns
  • Leadership development, mentoring, and peer mobilization
  • Exposure to policy dialogue and youth participation strategies
  • Access to a nationwide network of changemakers and mentors

Key Responsibilities

As a member of the Young People’s Action Team, you will:

  • Represent youth voices in governance and policy discussions
  • Lead and support community-based initiatives
  • Organize and participate in youth-focused events and dialogues
  • Create impactful digital content for social change
  • Mentor peers and mobilize youth networks
  • Participate in UNICEF-led campaigns and training programs

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for this opportunity, applicants must:

  • Be a Nigerian citizen
  • Be between 10 and 29 years old
  • Be passionate about youth empowerment and social impact
  • Be willing to commit to a 12-month program (both online and in-person)
  • Have access to a smartphone and internet connection
  • Be proficient in English

How to Apply

Interested candidates can submit their application through the official link below. The application process takes approximately 20–30 minutes to complete.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more volunteering opportunities, click here. 

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted on or before 25 April 2026. Early submission is strongly encouraged, as no deadline extension will be granted.

Final Thoughts

The UNICEF YPAT program is more than a volunteer role—it is a platform to amplify your voice, build your skills, and contribute to meaningful change. If you are ready to step into leadership, influence policy, and empower your peers, this is your moment.

Do not sit on the sidelines. Step into the room and take action toward shaping a better future.

The post Join UNICEF Nigeria’s Youth Action Team (YPAT) 2026: A Life-Changing Opportunity for Young Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Write Your Way to Global Impact: Apply for the 2026 Emerging Perspectives Short Essay Competition (Win $1,000 + Publication)
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The 2026 Emerging Perspectives Short Essay Competition is officially open, offering young writers a powerful platform to share their voices on one of the most pressing global issues of our time—migration in the age of polycrisis. Organized by the Mixed Migration Centre, this annual competition invites fresh, original perspectives from emerging thinkers across the Global […]

The post Write Your Way to Global Impact: Apply for the 2026 Emerging Perspectives Short Essay Competition (Win $1,000 + Publication) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 Emerging Perspectives Short Essay Competition is officially open, offering young writers a powerful platform to share their voices on one of the most pressing global issues of our time—migration in the age of polycrisis.

Organized by the Mixed Migration Centre, this annual competition invites fresh, original perspectives from emerging thinkers across the Global South. If you have a story, insight, or lived experience about how overlapping global crises are shaping migration today, this is your opportunity to be heard globally—and rewarded for it.

Why This Competition Matters

We are living in an era defined by interconnected crises—climate change, conflict, economic instability, and geopolitical shifts. Together, these forces are transforming migration patterns worldwide.

The Mixed Migration Review 2026 (MMR 2026) will explore how this “polycrisis” is reshaping human mobility. This competition is your chance to contribute directly to that global conversation.

What You’ll Gain

Participating in this competition offers more than just recognition:

  • Global Publication: Winning essays will be featured in the prestigious Mixed Migration Review 2026
  • Cash Prize: Each of the five winners will receive USD $1,000
  • International Exposure: Share your voice with policymakers, researchers, and global audiences
  • Career Boost: Strengthen your portfolio as a writer, researcher, or storyteller

Competition Theme: Migration in the Age of Polycrisis

Applicants are encouraged to explore how overlapping global crises are influencing migration. Possible angles include:

  • How intersecting crises affect migration decisions and experiences
  • The politicization of migration in your region
  • Climate change as a driver of migration
  • Shifting migration policies and border control measures
  • Labour migration realities
  • Changes in humanitarian protection systems

Your submission should focus on original storytelling, personal insights, or underrepresented perspectives rather than academic theory.

Submission Format

The application process happens in two stages:

Stage 1: Abstract Submission

  • Submit a maximum 200-word abstract outlining your essay idea

Stage 2: Full Essay (if selected)

  • Selected applicants will be invited to submit a 1,500-word essay

Important Deadline

  • Abstract submission deadline: May 5, 2026
  • Invitations for full essays: May 22, 2026
  • Final essay submission: June 22, 2026
  • Winners announced: July 13, 2026

How to Apply

Submit your abstract through the official application form.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more competition opportunities, click here. 

Key Rules to Keep in Mind

  • Only one abstract per applicant is allowed
  • Co-authored essays are permitted (prize awarded per essay)
  • AI-generated content is strictly prohibited and will lead to disqualification
  • Essays must not exceed 1,500 words

What Makes a Winning Entry?

The selection team is looking for:

  • Authentic, personal, or locally grounded perspectives
  • Creative storytelling and compelling narratives
  • Fresh insights on underreported migration issues
  • Practical or innovative ideas for improving migration policies

Final Thoughts

This is more than a writing competition—it’s a chance to shape global understanding of migration through your lived experience and ideas. If you’ve ever wanted your voice to contribute to meaningful change, now is the time.

Submit your abstract, tell your story, and become part of a global dialogue that matters.

The post Write Your Way to Global Impact: Apply for the 2026 Emerging Perspectives Short Essay Competition (Win $1,000 + Publication) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Afrobarometer Assistant Project Manager Job in Ghana (2026) – Apply Before April 17
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
If you’re an experienced research professional with a passion for data-driven development across Africa, the Afrobarometer Assistant Project Manager role offers a unique opportunity to lead impactful survey projects across multiple countries. Based in Accra, Ghana, this full-time position places you at the heart of one of Africa’s most respected research networks. About the Role […]

The post Afrobarometer Assistant Project Manager Job in Ghana (2026) – Apply Before April 17 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you’re an experienced research professional with a passion for data-driven development across Africa, the Afrobarometer Assistant Project Manager role offers a unique opportunity to lead impactful survey projects across multiple countries. Based in Accra, Ghana, this full-time position places you at the heart of one of Africa’s most respected research networks.

About the Role

The Assistant Project Manager (APM) will oversee Afrobarometer’s regional survey operations in selected African countries. Working closely with senior leadership, including the Core Partner Project Manager and Survey Directorate, the APM ensures that all survey activities meet strict Afrobarometer protocols and timelines.

This role is ideal for professionals who thrive in project management, research coordination, and international collaboration.

Key Responsibilities

1. Survey Management

  • Coordinate with National Partners (NPs) to develop survey schedules
  • Review budgets and ensure cost-effectiveness
  • Manage sub-contracts and authorize payments
  • Review deliverables such as questionnaires, translations, and reports
  • Ensure compliance with Afrobarometer survey protocols
  • Provide technical support and training to partners

2. Dissemination & Communication

  • Oversee dissemination of survey findings
  • Promote research through workshops, conferences, and publications
  • Support press releases and global result launches

3. Network & Partnership Development

  • Evaluate and recruit new National Partners
  • Support special research projects
  • Participate in committees and strategic initiatives

4. Leadership & Strategy

  • Build stakeholder relationships across Africa
  • Contribute to Afrobarometer’s strategic growth
  • Encourage innovation and performance excellence

5. Monitoring & Reporting

  • Update monitoring and evaluation systems
  • Prepare technical and financial reports
  • Participate in advisory and survey unit meetings

6. Fundraising

  • Support local fundraising efforts for survey activities

Location

Accra, Ghana

Employment Type

Full-time

Required Qualifications

  • Postgraduate degree (preferably in social sciences)
  • Minimum of 3 years’ experience in survey research (NGO, public sector, or academia)
  • Strong analytical, research, and statistical skills (SPSS required)
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills
  • Proven project management experience
  • Ability to work in multicultural environments
  • Willingness to travel across Africa

Why Apply?

  • Work on high-impact research influencing policy across Africa
  • Gain international exposure and leadership experience
  • Collaborate with top researchers and institutions
  • Contribute to evidence-based governance and development

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their CV and cover letter via email: 📧 jobs@cddgh.org

Application Deadline

April 17, 2026

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more job opportunities, click here. 

The post Afrobarometer Assistant Project Manager Job in Ghana (2026) – Apply Before April 17 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
